title: Update mesh claim for persistent lane reuse and auto-selection
description: Change mesh claim to reuse existing lane worktrees instead of creating a new worktree per task. If the specified or auto-selected lane already has a worktree, reset it to wt/{workspace_id} (synced to origin/main) and create the task branch there. If no --lane is specified, auto-select the first idle lane from the coordination state. The claim atomic write (git commit + push to mesh/state) is the conflict guard — a push rejection means another agent claimed first; retry. Removes the per-task git worktree add call.
acceptance_criteria: mesh claim WORK-ID reuses the existing lane worktree if one exists for the selected workspace_id
Before starting work, the lane is reset to wt/{workspace_id} synced to origin/main
mesh claim auto-selects the first idle lane when --lane is not specified
Claim write is a git commit + push to mesh/state; push conflict triggers a retry with re-read
mesh claim still creates a new worktree if the lane has no existing worktree (first claim for that lane)
REQUIRED: cd output points to the existing lane path, not a newly created path
Tests cover: reuse existing lane, auto-select idle lane, conflict retry, first-time lane creation
